parsed.answer: Based on WHO and global alcohol consumption data, many countries consume less spirits than Italy (which consumes roughly 1-1.5 liters of pure alcohol from spirits per capita annually). These include most Muslim-majority countries (near zero), many Asian countries (e.g., Japan, South Korea consume similar or less from spirits specifically), and various nations in Africa, South Asia, and parts of Latin America. However, Eastern European countries (Russia, Poland, Czech Republic) and Nordic countries generally consume significantly MORE spirits than Italy. Without a specific dataset referenced by this question, a precise list is uncertain.
